A STORM DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 100 MILES PER HOUR TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHWEST EDMONSON COUNTY AT 540 PM CDT SATURDAY APRIL 24 2010. THE 50 YARD WIDE DAMAGE PATH STARTED NEAR SUNFISH AND TRAVELED EAST SOUTHEAST FOR 1.1 MILES. A WELL BUILT HOME SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ALONG WITH A SHED THAT WAS DESTROYED. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED AND NUMEROUS HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED.

CONFIRMED EF-1 TORNADO. MAX ESTIMATED WINDS 90 MPH. TOUCH DOWN AROUND 825 PM EDT NEAR MCAFEE ALONG RT 127 IN MERCER COUNTY. IT TRAVELED 1.4 MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR EBENEZER. MOSTLY RATED EF-0... BUT EF-1 DAMAGE OBSERVED NEAR PATTERSON LANE AND MCAFEE LANE WHERE A WELL BUILT BARN HAD 2 WALLS BLOWN OUT AND ITS ROOF BLOWN ONE-HALF MILE DOWNSTREAM. TWO OTHER BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. A FEW HARDWOOD TREES ALSO WERE UPROOTED OR DAMAGED. PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

WEAK EF-0 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY NWS DAMAGE SURVEY. TOUCHDOWN 1 MILE SOUTHEAST OF BIG SPRINGS IN EASTERN BRECKINRIDGE COUNTY. TORNADO DISSIPATED 1.6 MILES SOUTHEAST OF BIG SPRING IN WESTERN HARDIN COUNTY.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY ALONG COUNTY ROAD 2199 IN FAR EASTERN BRECKINRIDGE COUNTY. IT THEN LIFTED AND TOUCHED DOWN AGAIN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 220 IN FAR NORTHWESTERN HARDIN COUNTY WHERE IT SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED A DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME AND ATTACHED GARAGE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 80 TO 85 MPH WITH A PATH LENGTH OF THREE-QUARTERS OF A MILE AND A PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EF-0 TORNADO DAMAGE ALONG BOGARD LANE JUST SOUTH OF HWY 44 BETWEEN SHEPHERDSVILLE AND MT. WASHINGTON. PATH LENGTH WAS 150 YARDS. PATH WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS. WELL-CONSTRUCTED CONCRETE WORKSHOP BUILDING WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED WITH 3-4000 POUND ROOF BLOWN 45 YARDS AWAY. A 125 POUND CHIMNEY WAS ALSO BLOWN 60 YARDS AWAY. INSULATION WAS FOUND IN TWISTED TREES. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS AND BRANCHES DESTROYED.
